description: The lemon is a species of small evergreen tree in the Citrus genus of the flowering plant family Rutaceae. A true lemon is a hybrid of the citron and the bitter orange. Its origins are uncertain, but some evidence suggests lemons originated during the 1st millennium BC in what is now northeastern India. Some other citrus fruits are called lemon.

care.water_frequency: Every 5-7 days, keep soil evenly moist
care.humidity_action: Mist during flowering to help pollination. Good air circulation.
care.repot_frequency: Every 2-3 years, one size up only
care.fertilizer_type: Citrus-specific fertilizer every 2 weeks in growing season
care.tips: Indoor trees need hand-pollination with a brush. Cold period (10-13°C) in winter triggers blooming. Prune to maintain shape.
care.toxicity_note: Essential oils and psoralens toxic to cats and dogs

description: Fruits are modified berries (hesperidium), dull lemon-yellow like a small orange, fragrant when ripe, sour, many-seeded with thick peel and oil glands. The peel is also rough and slightly fuzzy. Though sour, the juice can be used to flavor beverages. It can be used to make marmalade.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_problems: No serious pest or disease problems.
It can be weedy and is considered
common_pests: No serious pest or disease problems.

description: Star-shaped seed heads can be left on the plant after flowering to provide some ornamental interest.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

care.tips: Good companions: Red raspberry, Rose | Keep apart: Basil, Sage

description: Zanthoxylum americanum, the common prickly-ash, common pricklyash, common prickly ash or northern prickly-ash, is an aromatic shrub or small tree native to central and eastern portions of the United States and Canada. It is the northernmost New World species in the citrus family, Rutaceae, and is the type species in its genus, which includes sichuan pepper. It can grow to 10 meters (33 ft) tall with a diameter at breast height (DBH) of 15 cm (5.9 in).
Zanthoxylum clava-herculis, the Hercules' club, Hercules-club, pepperwood, or southern prickly ash, is a spiny tree or shrub native to the southeastern United States.
